chief_complaint: Two patients presented with acute coronary syndrome, one with continued chest pain despite medical therapy and the other with plaque progression.
history: Both patients had coronary artery disease. The first patient experienced chest pain despite receiving medical therapy, leading to the detection of plaque ulceration. The second patient had two photon-counting computed tomography (PCCT) screenings within 6 months, revealing plaque progression.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are reported.
labs: Photon-counting computed tomography (PCCT) and intravascular ultrasound (IVUS) were used for diagnosis and intervention guidance.
